query_normalized: predicting phase transitions in amorphous solids using machine learning chemistry
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by providing evidence of the same physical mechanism (information compression/entropy minimization) occurring at a phase transition, even though the specific system (cold atom gases) and state (turbulent vs. amorphous) differ. It also meets criterion (d) as a foundational reference for using information-theoretic metrics (Shannon entropy) to detect phase transitions in non-equilibrium physical systems, which is directly relevant to the user's goal of applying m
